channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Top-tier college student, former high school football star killed in freak accident
item.description: Authorities confirmed that a Clemson University student died after he was crushed and trapped by a fallen tree in a freak accident over the weekend in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump makes moves to expand power, sparking chaos, possible constitutional crisis
item.description: Just a little over a week into his second term, President Donald Trump is taking steps to maximize his power, sparking chaos and what critics contend is a constitutional crisis as he challenges the separation of powers that have defined American government for more than 200 years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top NYPD officials faulted for 'demeaning,' 'unprofessional' social media posts attacking critics
item.description: Two NYPD chiefs violated department policy when they made "demeaning" social media posts to attack civil servants and journalists, an independent monitor found.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A $4B settlement for Hawaii wildfire victims is in legal limbo as an unusual trial starts
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — When Hawaii Gov. Josh Green announced a $4 billion settlement about a year after the deadliest U.S. wildfire in a century devastated Lahaina in 2023, he touted the speed of the deal to “avoid protracted and painful lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Flawed emergency alert systems lagged when residents needed them most during Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: When disaster strikes, government emergency alert systems offer a simple promise: Residents will get information about nearby dangers and instructions to help them stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Christopher L. Keller, Claudia Lauer, Amy Taxin And Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Texas Gov. Abbott orders DPS tactical strike teams to assist federal illegal immigration enforcement efforts
item.description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott ordered DPS tactical strike teams to assist the Trump administration's efforts to go after illegal immigrants inside the state, as Austin law enforcement officials vow to not comply.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, GEOFF MULVIHILL and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Weeks after fire ravaged LA's Pacific Palisades, residents return to dig for mementos
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Scores of Los Angeles residents returned Tuesday to wildfire-ravaged Pacific Palisades after officials began letting people drive back into the scorched community, the first opportunity most have had to carefully scour the sites of their former homes.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Head Start and Medicaid providers hit glitches as Trump freezes federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As President Donald Trump paused federal grants and loans in a far-reaching directive, early childhood education centers and states discovered Tuesday they could no longer access money they rely on to provide care for some of the nation's neediest families and children.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A$AP Rocky's accuser says he was stunned and furious when his old friend pulled a gun on him
item.description: A$AP Rocky's accuser, former friend and the key witness at his trial testified Tuesday that their relationship had been fraying for years, but he was “furious” and flabbergasted when Rocky pulled a gun on him on the streets of Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Young people 'inspired' by Trump and Musk, UCLA survey finds
item.description: (KTLA) -- A large number of young people say they are "inspired" by the likes of Elon Musk and Donald Trump and want to be like them, a survey from UCLA found.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Sisters convicted in planning murder-for-hire scheme
item.description: FAIRHOPE, Ala. (WKRG) — Two Fairhope sisters were convicted on multiple charges in a murder-for-hire scheme, according to the United States Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Pat O'Donnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US education agency investigating Denver schools over all-gender bathroom
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The U.S. Education Department said Tuesday it was investigating Denver Public Schools for alleged discrimination after the district converted a girl’s restroom on the second floor of a high school into an all-gender restroom while leaving another bathroom exclusive to boys.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Homeland Security secretary joins federal agents to tout immigrant arrests in NYC
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem joined federal agents Tuesday in New York City to announce the arrests of immigrants wanted on criminal charges, including a man accused of kidnapping who was seen on a viral video entering a Colorado apartment with reputed gang members.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House press briefing discusses federal assistance, deportation
item.description: Karoline Leavitt addressed the media from the White House's briefing room for the first time on Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: A worker at Charlotte Douglas International Airport (CLT) died Monday after being struck by an airline ramp vehicle on the tarmac, local police have confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, Fox 5

item.title: Shooting at Indiana grocery store leaves 3 dead, including the suspect, and 2 officers wounded
item.description: When police “engaged” the shooting suspect outside the store, two officers were wounded and the suspect was fatally shot, according to the Elkhart Police Department.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Indiana man pardoned by Trump is fatally shot during a traffic stop
item.description: RENSSELAER, Ind. (AP) — An Indiana man was fatally shot by a deputy during a traffic stop just days after he was pardoned by President Donald Trump for a misdemeanor related to the 2021 U.S. Capitol riot.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano is spewing lava once again
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world’s most active volcanoes is back in action. The Kilauea volcano at Hawaii Volcanoes National Park is spewing lava once again, the seventh recorded episode in recent weeks. The eruption that began Dec. 23 in a crater at Kilauea's summit has paused periodically.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family, friends of Gabby Petito share details surrounding her death in upcoming Netflix documentary
item.description: An upcoming Netflix documentary will shed new light on the story of Gabby Petito and her death at the hands of her fiancé, Brian Laundrie.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ fires prosecutors who worked on Trump criminal cases
item.description: Justice Department officials fired several prosecutors who worked on President Trump's criminal cases, saying they could not “trust” them.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Woman gets more than 3-year prison term for helping pregnant Chinese women get to US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California woman was sentenced Monday to more than 3 years in prison in a long-running case over a business that helped pregnant Chinese women travel to the United States to deliver babies who automatically became American citizens. U.S. District Judge R.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by Trump shot, killed by sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, KRON4

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Trump's first executive actions on climate and environment
item.description: President Donald Trump's first week in office included a flurry of executive orders with implications for Earth's climate and environment.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mel Gibson’s ‘Flight Risk’ is No. 1 at box office, ‘The Brutalist’ expands
item.description: Critics lambasted it and audiences didn’t grade it much better. But despite the turbulence, Mel Gibson’s “Flight Risk” managed to open No. 1 at the box office with a modest $12 million, according to studio estimates Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What is a migrant? What is ICE? 10 terms to help you understand the debate over immigration
item.description: Understanding the many different immigration terms – some technical, some not – can help people better understand immigration news.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Castañeda Professor of Latin American and Latino studies, American University, Fox 5

item.title: Black History Month movie celebration at AMC Theatres
item.description: The movie theater chain will bring back seven fan-favorite films from the past year for the month of February. Tickets for each show are already available to purchase online.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, Fox 5

item.title: Jets agree to terms with Broncos executive Darren Mougey to be their general manager
item.description: The New York Jets' lengthy searches are over, and they're pairing a first-time general manager with a first-time head coach to try to turn things around.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK JR., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Private investigator first to admit guilt in offering bribes to get intoxicated drivers off the hook
item.description: A private investigator embroiled in a corruption scandal that rocked New Mexico’s law enforcement community for over a decade was the first to plead guilty to a slew of federal charges that include racketeering and bribery, according to court records released Friday.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s bid to end birthright citizenship sparks GOP divisions
item.description: President Trump’s bid to end birthright citizenship is dividing House Republicans, highlighting internal differences on the thorny topic of immigration just as the GOP is making gains with Hispanic voters.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Freighter remains stuck in the ice on a frozen Lake Erie
item.description: The U.S. and Canadian Coast Guards on Saturday continued efforts to break up ice and free a freighter that has been trapped in a frozen Lake Erie for days, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ozempic and similar weight loss drugs may lower risk of 42 health conditions, but also pose risks
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration’s approval of Ozempic in 2021 for weight loss treatment ushered in a new era for the class of drugs called glucagon-like peptide-1 agonists, or GLP-1.
item.dc:creator: Ziyad Al-Aly Clinical Epidemiologist, Washington University in St. Louis, Fox 5

item.title: China tells Rubio to behave himself in veiled warning
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's veteran foreign minister has issued a veiled warning to America's new secretary of state: Behave yourself.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, ABC 4
